items.title: Why Claude Fable 5 Was Just Forced Offline
items.channel: AI News & Strategy Daily | Nate B Jones
items.summary: Analysis of the US export-control takedown. Three layers: a real safety concern (a jailbreak likely generalizes across frontier models), a 'foreign nationals' legal fig-leaf that in practice = a full shutdown, and business reality → he expects it resolved soon.
items.relevance: You run a Claude-based agent team and the ban hit you personally — his 'never depend on one model/lab/country' point is a direct ops-risk lesson.

items.title: Anthropic is starting to panic…
items.summary: Anthropic passes OpenAI in valuation + files a trillion-$ IPO, yet proposes a global AI 'pause' over recursive self-improvement (conveniently freezing its lead). Cites the 'AI Layoff Trap' paper and MIT's 95%-of-AI-projects-zero-ROI finding.
items.relevance: The IPO/'pause' politics behind the Fable ban you're living through. 5-min context.

items.title: Anthropic begged the world to stop AI… then shipped this
items.summary: Fable = Mythos + a safety 'muzzle': $50/M output tokens vs $25 Opus; classifiers block cyber/bio/chem/distillation and route those to Opus; free on paid plans till Jun 22. Strong reviews (Bend creator's 'singularity moment').
items.relevance: Explains Fable's price + muzzle vs Opus — you build with Claude Code daily, so this is your tooling.

items.title: I Made Opus 4.8 and Fable 5 Build the Same App (RAW RESULTS)
items.summary: One-shot builds of an e-commerce store, a 3D art-history museum, and an Age of Empires clone. Fable 5 won all three — faster, more token-efficient (cheaper net despite higher price), better taste, working interactivity where Opus broke.
items.relevance: Direct comparison of the exact models your agents run; the token-efficiency speaks to your build costs.

items.title: Claude Fable 5 UI/UX One-Shots - 5 Tests
items.summary: Five one-shot UI generations (award portfolio, 3JS gallery, hero redesign, Craigslist modernization, recreate an award site). Calls it the best one-shot UI he's seen; argues taste still separates the top 10% of designers.
items.relevance: One-shot UI quality — useful for razbakov.com, Web100 client sites, and rapid mockups.

items.title: My Take on The New Apple
items.summary: Tim Cook → chairman; John Ternus (a hardware/product guy) becomes CEO in Sept, capping a coordinated generational handover of Apple's C-suite. Hopes for bolder hardware (folding iPhone) over the services drift; worries Apple plays too safe.
items.relevance: Apple shifting to a product CEO — context for betting Loci on Apple's near-term roadmap.

items.title: Is Apple Vision Pro Dead? I Asked Apple's CFO
items.summary: Interview with CFO Kevan Parekh at NUS Singapore. Frames AVP as a long-term ecosystem play in 'early innings' — real traction in surgical training, education, and design/HVAC visualization. Message: not dead, developer-first.
items.relevance: Apple's own framing of AVP as long-term — a signal for your Loci/VPG path.

items.title: Siri AI in Vision Pro - First Look
items.summary: Hands-on with visionOS 27 dev-beta Siri: LLM-based, contextual across iMessage/photos/calendar, sees through your gaze, movable orb. Nails some queries (flight/hotel context, plant ID), misses many (group texts, bill-split). Promising but beta-rough.
items.relevance: Literally the surface you're building Loci on (Foundation Models, App Intents, spatial).

items.title: Why you don't really exist | Sam Harris, Roger Penrose, Sophie Scott
items.channel: The Institute of Art and Ideas
items.summary: Panel on the self. Harris: the 'I' behind experience is an illusion (though consciousness itself can't be); Penrose: ties self to consciousness, split-brain complicates it; Scott: self = brain network + memory + social modeling.
items.relevance: The self as illusion — feeds Agora's 'personal sovereignty' layer and your dual-register Loci thinking.

items.title: What's Outside The Simulation w/ Donald Hoffman?
items.summary: Hoffman: spacetime isn't fundamental ('doomed' at the Planck scale); reality is conscious agents + Markov chains, and science could find the 'code' outside the headset and edit it → magic-like tech. Bilyeu counters: we're computational NPCs with no exit and no free will. Long, speculative.
items.relevance: Consciousness-as-fundamental + agents — aligns with your Agora + AI-governance worldview.

items.title: If You Hack The Code Of Reality, You Become God | Dr Melvin Vopson
items.summary: Vopson's 'information physics': information as a 5th state of matter with mass; his 'second law of infodynamics' (the universe compresses/optimizes → symmetry, error-correcting codes in string theory) as evidence reality is computational; dark matter as the 'code'; a fly-brain emulation as proof life can be simulated. Heavy speculation (incl. reading John 1:1 as 'God is an AI').
items.relevance: Information physics + simulation — same philosophical lane as Agora / liquid democracy.

items.title: Weird Things Happen When You Look At The Gaps Between Primes
items.summary: The twin-prime story: Brun's sieve → Yitang Zhang's 2013 breakthrough (bounded gap of 70M, done in obscurity) → Maynard/Tao/Polymath pushing it to 246. The 'one-half barrier' was a mirage.
items.relevance: No project tie — pure enjoyment, plus the 'ignoring impossible is how it got solved' meta-lesson.
